response.justification: Candidate is a strong full-stack software craftsman with deep web/backend experience (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) but lacks the core qualifications for a Principal Performance Architect role focused on AI accelerator hardware. No evidence of SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, deep neural network optimization, GPU/SIMD/MIMD architecture experience, or advanced degree typically expected. Brief C/C++ work at National Instruments is the closest adjacency but is over a decade old and was IDE tooling, not hardware modeling.
strengths: Long IC career with broad systems exposure including low-latency, high-throughput services at Sociomantic
Some C/C++ experience at National Instruments on engineering measurement tooling
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, performance-conscious work in DLang
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator, or hardware-software co-design experience
No deep learning, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or DNN optimization background
Educational path (BS CS) without the depth of hardware/architecture work typically required at Principal level for this domain

key_factors.factor: Missing AI accelerator / SoC modeling background
No deep learning or GPU architecture experience
